Padded.special_description: Light armor. Quilted layers of cloth and batting. No gauntlets or other accessories included by default.

Leather.special_description: Light armor made from hardened leather. Flexible and quiet.

Studded Leather.special_description: Light armor. Hardened leather reinforced with metal studs or small plates.

Chain Shirt.special_description: Light armor. A shirt of chain links, typically worn under normal clothing. Comes with a steel cap.

Hide.special_description: Medium armor. Thick, stiff hides and furs. Noisy and smelly but cheap and effective.

Scale Mail.special_description: Medium armor. Metal scales sewn or riveted to a leather backing.

Chainmail.special_description: Medium armor. Standard chain links with a padded underlayer. The suit includes gauntlets.

Breastplate.special_description: Medium armor. A fitted breastplate of steel or similar. Comes with a helmet and greaves. Good balance of protection and mobility.

Splint Mail.special_description: Heavy armor. Narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a leather or cloth backing. When running in heavy armor you move only triple speed.

Banded Mail.special_description: Heavy armor. Horizontal strips of metal (bands) over chain or leather. The suit includes gauntlets. When running in heavy armor you move only triple speed.

Half-Plate.special_description: Heavy armor. Partial plate covering vital areas over a chain shirt or similar. Excellent protection. When running in heavy armor you move only triple speed.

Full Plate.special_description: Heavy armor. The best standard armor. A complete suit of interlocking metal plates. Includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick padding underneath. Each suit must be individually fitted (resizing a captured suit costs 200-800 gp). When running in heavy armor you move only triple speed.

Buckler.special_description: Small metal shield strapped to the forearm. You can use a bow or crossbow without penalty while carrying it. You can wield a weapon in the shield arm but take -1 on attack rolls (stacks with two-weapon penalties). You don’t get the buckler’s AC bonus the rest of the round if you attack with an off-hand weapon. You cannot bash with a buckler.

Shield, light wooden.special_description: Light shield made of wood. Can be used to make a shield bash attack (see weapons for damage). Hand not free to cast spells while bashing or using actively.

Shield, light steel.special_description: Light shield made of steel. More durable than wooden equivalent. Can be used to make a shield bash attack.

Shield, heavy wooden.special_description: Heavy shield made of wood. Greater protection than light shields. Can be used to bash.

Shield, heavy steel.special_description: Heavy shield made of steel. Standard heavy shield. Can be used to bash. Hand not free to cast spells.

Shield, tower.special_description: A massive shield that can provide cover (+4 to AC or better vs. ranged in some cases, or actual cover vs. attacks). You can use it to grant yourself cover as a standard action (or move?). Very high arcane failure and check penalty. Footnote: can grant cover instead of +4 shield bonus in some positioning.
